title: [InstCombine] Match range check pattern with SExt

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_sext_i16_to_i32(i16 %x, i32 %n) {
  %nn = and i32 %n, 32767
  %x_sext = sext i16 %x to i32
  %a = icmp sge i16 %x, 0
  %b = icmp slt i32 %x_sext, %nn
  %c = and i1 %a, %b
  ret i1 %c
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @test_sext_i16_to_i32(i16 %x, i32 %n) {
  %nn = and i32 %n, 32767
  %a = icmp sge i16 %x, 0
  %b = icmp slt i32 %x, %nn
  %c = and i1 %a, %b
  ret i1 %c
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only mentions i32 to i64 sign-extension, but patch supports any width combination via m_SExtOrSelf matcher (i16 to i32 not explicitly documented)

alive2.src:
define i1 @test_sext_i16_to_i32(i16 %x, i32 %n) {
  %nn = and i32 %n, 32767
  %x_sext = sext i16 %x to i32
  %a = icmp sge i16 %x, 0
  %b = icmp slt i32 %x_sext, %nn
  %c = and i1 %a, %b
  ret i1 %c
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @test_sext_i16_to_i32(i16 %x, i32 %n) {
  %nn = and i32 %n, 32767
  %x_sext = sext i16 %x to i32
  %c = icmp ugt i32 %nn, %x_sext
  ret i1 %c
}

define i1 @test_sext_i8_to_i64_wide_range(i8 %x, i64 %n) {
  %nn = and i64 %n, 127
  %x_sext = sext i8 %x to i64
  %a = icmp sge i8 %x, 0
  %b = icmp slt i64 %x_sext, %nn
  %c = and i1 %a, %b
  ret i1 %c
}

  %b = icmp slt i64 %x, %nn
  %c = and i1 %a, %b

}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary only documents i32-to-i64 extension pattern; i8-to-i64 cross-width sign-extension is supported by matcher but not mentioned

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_sext_both_comparisons(i32 %x, i64 %n) {
  %nn = and i64 %n, 2147483647
  %x_sext1 = sext i32 %x to i64
  %x_sext2 = sext i32 %x to i64
  %a = icmp sge i64 %x_sext1, 0
  %b = icmp slt i64 %x_sext2, %nn
  %c = and i1 %a, %b
  ret i1 %c
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
define i1 @test_sext_both_comparisons(i32 %x, i64 %n) {
  %nn = and i64 %n, 2147483647
  %a = icmp sge i64 %x, 0
  %b = icmp slt i64 %x, %nn
  %c = and i1 %a, %b
  ret i1 %c
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes sext on one comparison operand; patch handles cases where both comparisons use separate sext instructions from same input
